---
title: |
  | Problem Set 6
author: "Ryan T. Moore"
date: "(date)"
output: 
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: true
bibliography: ../../admin/main.bib    
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Multiarm Bandits 

a)  Demonstrate how treatment arms' posterior probabilities of being the best
become more and less certain as the number of trials change in absolute terms
and in relative terms. See the code provided in `06-bandits.R` for help.

b) Replicate the three left panels of Figure 1 from @offcopgre21. See the code provided in `06-bandits.R` for help.

c) Suppose that financial and IRB constraints are not binding. Describe a political or policy experiment that you would like to run via a multiarm bandit.

# References
